frameworks.name: parallel processing
frameworks.one_line: Form features processed simultaneously across early visual areas rather than only hierarchically.

frameworks.name: hierarchical visual processing
frameworks.one_line: Traditional view that complexity builds across successive cortical stages.

core_question: Are simple geometric forms of increasing perceptual complexity processed hierarchically or in parallel across early visual areas?
key_assumptions: Lines, angles, and rhombuses share the same elemental constituents (lines)
Perceptual complexity increases from lines to angles to rhombuses
BOLD activity in V1-V3 indexes form-related processing
manipulations: Form category (lines vs angles vs rhombuses)
Perceptual complexity of geometric stimuli built from identical line elements
key_findings: All three form categories activated V1, V2, and V3
Angles produced the strongest activity; rhombuses the weakest, across V1-V3
Difference between angles and rhombuses was significant; lines vs rhombuses trend-significant; lines vs angles non-significant
interpretations: Form processing uses a parallel strategy across early visual areas in addition to the hierarchical one
Complementing earlier MEG evidence, fMRI localizes parallel form-related activity to V1-V3
